HOME REAL DECISIONS WHAT WE BUILD THE PROCESS

For those who make the final call

Built for the decisions you only get to make once.

It Knows You
Most AI conversations start from scratch. Yours doesn’t. It learns your people, priorities, and the way you work, so every interaction begins with context instead of guesswork.
You Stay in Control
AI can provide insight. It can’t provide judgment. The system organizes information, highlights opportunities, and flags risks. The decisions remain yours.
It Improves Over Time
The more you use it, the more useful it becomes. It learns from your business, adapts to your preferences, and delivers increasingly relevant support over time.

What We Build

Memory Architecture
Context that never resets. Your consultations, test results, research, and correspondence live in one persistent system, purpose-built for your situation. Every session starts with your full history already loaded.
Decision Council
A multi-agent council of specialized roles, each injected with domain-specific intelligence relevant to your situation. Not to replace your judgment. Not to give you medical or legal advice. But to surface dimensions you might otherwise miss, and show you exactly what each perspective brings before you synthesize.
Decision Architecture
This is where complexity becomes clarity. The council’s perspectives are structured into a single governing framework: your tradeoffs made visible, your priorities ranked, your path forward named. You don’t leave with more information. You leave with a decision. The system is your cognitive exoskeleton: it augments your thinking, but the decision, and everything that follows, is yours.
It Teaches You
Most AI tools are frozen the day you buy them. Yours learns and grows the more you use it. Every system includes an intelligence layer that keeps you in command while teaching you to direct it better: how to use AI well, ask sharper questions, and grow more fluent every session. You don’t stay dependent on us.
It Builds With You
Need a new council for a new decision? The system walks you through it step by step, architecting the prompt with our proprietary framework and configuring the databases behind it. Your system grows as your life does, without starting from scratch or waiting on us.
It Maintains and Gets Sharper
Run maintenance on command or on a weekly schedule. The system diagnoses and fixes most issues itself, and drafts an escalation email to AVANT GARDE when it can’t. Every session writes to your memory, so the system’s judgment about your world deepens the longer you own it. This is the difference between using AI and owning a system: you own the data, you own the decisions, and the intelligence is the leverage.

Real Decisions

Strategic Planning
A Philanthropist
A philanthropist and founder at 72, on a global walkabout, racing to capture a lifetime of decisions before the window closes. The council travels with him now, wherever the walkabout goes.

A Father and His Son
An economist by training, used to navigating complexity, yet facing the one challenge no framework had prepared him for: raising his autistic son. The council gave him what expertise alone could not.

Medical Decision-Making
A Retired Executive
A former executive navigating prostate surgery. Multiple specialists. Conflicting recommendations. Permanent consequences. The council became the second opinion that synthesized them all.
Surgical Decision Council →
Case 3 of 3

When Your Doctors Don’t Agree

The Problem

A retired executive in his late 50s, with an enlarged prostate, faced a decision he couldn’t afford to get wrong. He saw multiple specialists. Each one recommended a different procedure. Appointments were short. Questions went unanswered. So he did what everyone does. He went to Google. What he found was outdated, conflicting, and impossible to evaluate without a medical degree.

He had more information than ever and less clarity than when he started.

The System

We built a multi-role medical intelligence council that did what his doctors didn’t have time to do: aggregate everything (test results, clinical research, consultation summaries, specialist recommendations) into one clear picture.

One role flagged cancer-safety concerns, so he knew to raise it with his doctor.

The council then worked through every option (medications, minimally invasive procedures, and surgery), including the ~70% erectile dysfunction risk associated with TURP that no specialist had made fully explicit. No role could advocate for a procedure. Every claim was evidence-graded.

What he walked out with: a Physician Conversation Guide. Structured questions he could bring into his next appointment, so that the thirty minutes he had with his doctor actually moved the decision forward.

The Outcome

He stopped Googling. He walked into his next appointment knowing exactly what to ask and why. He understood the irreversible consequences of each path before he chose one. He made the call. He owned it.

Facing a Decision Your Doctors Can’t Agree On?

What you just saw was one council inside a larger system: a custom AI operating system built around you. It holds your full history, gives you specialist-level thinking whenever you need it, and grows as your needs do. Let’s get on a discovery call. You bring the decision you’re wrestling with; we’ll map what a system built for your world could look like. No commitment, no pressure.

Book Your Discovery Call
Case 2 of 3

The Father Who Needed a Framework for His Son

The Problem

The father is an economist by training. He analyzes complexity, builds frameworks, and solves hard problems, and he brings that same intentionality to raising his son.

His son is autistic. Like any child on the spectrum, he can have moments: an outburst, a sudden emotional spiral, behavior that seems to come from nowhere. For a father coming home depleted from work and the regular toils of life, those moments land hard. They elicit emotion. Frustration. Sometimes anger. Not because the father lacks love or commitment, but because no amount of professional training prepares you to decode a moment like that in real time.

The specialists who could help were appointment-gated: an hour here, weeks apart, each session starting from zero. What the father needed was something no appointment could give him: the ability to take in what just happened, understand it scientifically, and respond with poise instead of reaction.

The System

We built a multi-role council serving two functions: a structured space for the father to process the weight he carries, and a specialist team to help him decode his son’s world.

Every session opens with the Reflective Therapist. Before any guidance, the council gives the father room to set down the day, because a depleted father deserves support too, and a clear mind absorbs better guidance.

Then the specialists go to work: a Psychologist and Behavioral Analyst decode what triggered the moment and what the science says about it. A Parenting Expert and Performance Coach translate that understanding into response strategies. A Habit Specialist and Educational Strategist build the routines and structures that reduce the triggers over time. A Cultural Advisor grounds everything in the family’s cultural values. And the Synthesis Architect closes every session with the actionable output: what the father does next, and the understanding of why.

This is what no therapist’s office could offer: unlimited questions, answered at depth, at 2am if that is when he needs them. And because the council’s memory architecture holds everything (every incident, every breakthrough, every pattern), it never analyzes a moment in isolation. It sees the full picture over time. The patterns a once-a-month specialist could never catch.

The Outcome

Understanding gave the father the ability to plan. His son’s moments became legible: what triggers them, how to de-escalate them, and how to build routines that minimize them. The father now walks into those moments armed with data and poise instead of frustration.

That understanding traveled: he brought his findings to his son’s mother, who saw the value immediately, giving both parents a shared framework to move forward with their son.

And most importantly, his son is learning to regulate himself. The routines, the patterns, the structures are equipping him with the self-control and resilience he will need to face the world on his own terms. A deeper relationship between father and son. A plan that empowers him for the world.

See What a Council Could Do for Your Family

The council that helped this father was one part of something larger: a custom AI operating system built around his world, always on, always learning. Yours would be too: holding your full context, ready whenever you need it, growing as your life does. Let’s get on a discovery call. You bring the situation you’re navigating; we’ll map what a system built for you could look like. No commitment, no pressure.

Book Your Discovery Call
Case 1 of 3

A Life Worth Documenting

The Problem

He built everything from nothing. Born into hardship. His father died when he was four. Raised in the projects. In trouble with the law as a teenager. He made the decision to leave. Through college, graduate school, and a life lived across continents, he eventually built a philanthropic fund designed to outlive him.

At 72, he went on a global walkabout to write his memoir. The problem: a lifetime of decisions, principles, relationships, and hard-won wisdom lived entirely in his head. His sons were preparing to take over the Fund. His memoir was stalled. The institutional knowledge that made the Fund coherent had no home outside of the founder himself.

Without a system to capture it, the next generation might inherit the structure but would definitely lose the reasoning behind it.

The System

We built a multi-role Walkabout and Memoir Intelligence Council: not a ghostwriter, not a publishing consultant, but a persistent AI architecture that travels with him wherever the walkabout takes him.

The council holds everything: manuscript drafts, editorial analysis, Fund strategy, family governance decisions, giving philosophy, and the principles behind every major choice he has made. A dual-mandate agent ensures every decision about the memoir simultaneously serves the Philanthropic Fund.

An Editorial Intelligence Agent benchmarks every chapter against the top twenty published memoirs and surfaces what is working, what is underwritten, and what the transformation arc requires. A Market Validation Agent pressure-tests commercial viability in real time. Nothing gets written in the wrong direction for forty thousand words without someone noticing.

The council does not write the memoir. It makes sure he writes the right one.

The Outcome

The Fund’s governing logic is documented. The memoir has a structure: a thesis, a transformation arc, a frame built around the walkabout itself. He goes into every writing session with a council that remembers everything he has ever told it and knows exactly where the manuscript needs to go next.

He is building something that will outlive him. The system makes sure nothing critical gets lost before he finishes.

Legacy Isn’t What You Leave. It’s What Keeps Running Without You.

His council was one expression of a larger system: a custom AI operating system that captures how you think, governs what you’ve built, and carries your reasoning forward. It’s not a project. It’s infrastructure for everything that comes next. Let’s get on a discovery call. You bring what you’re trying to build, preserve, or pass on; we’ll map what a system built around your legacy could look like. No commitment, no pressure.

Book Your Discovery Call

Your Discovery Call

What happens next

We explore your situation, map the complexity, and design a system tailored to your world. We give you a clear architecture and a quote. Once you approve, we deploy hands-on and stay with you until it runs smoothly.

How long does it take?

Typically two to four weeks from discovery to full deployment, depending on complexity. You’ll have a clear timeline before you commit to anything.

What does it cost?

Cost depends on complexity. You’ll get a specific quote during the call, after we understand your situation, not before.

Book Your Discovery Call

No commitment. No pressure. You own the call.
